meta.siteTitle: Mark Watson for Oregon House District 29
meta.siteDescription: Mark Watson is running for Oregon House District 29, representing Hillsboro, Forest Grove, and Cornelius. Small business owner, school board director, and community advocate.

footer.siteHeading: Mark Watson for Oregon House District 29
footer.paidFor: Paid for by Friends of Mark Watson

home.name: Mark Watson
home.quote: I have lived in Washington County for more than 25 years–I've raised my family here, operated a successful small business, and proudly served on the Hillsboro School Board since 2017. I am running for State Representative for House District 29 because our community needs a leader who listens, acts with empathy, and is ready to get to work for our residents during these challenging times.
home.rootedText: After 26 years in Oregon, the Watson family has deep roots in Washington County. From raising children in the Hillsboro School District to now living in a multigenerational household in Downtown Hillsboro, Mark understands the needs of local families.
home.schoolBoardText: Advocating for students and teachers in the Hillsboro School District
home.legislativeAideText: Working in the State Capitol to serve District 29 residents
home.smallBusinessText: Co-founded a software company serving MLB and NHL teams
home.joinText: Your contribution helps bring experienced, community-focused leadership to the Oregon House.

about.heroSubtitle: Husband, father, small business owner, school board director, and your neighbor in Washington County.
about.bio1: Mark is a husband, father, small business owner, and school board director. He has spent all of his time in Oregon living in the Hillsboro School District and recently moved to Downtown Hillsboro.
about.bio2: In 2002 Mark co-founded TVTI, a software company, specializing in computerized video analysis for MLB and NHL teams, securing contracts with almost all teams.
about.bio3: Recently he has been working as a legislative aide in the State Capitol, serving offices in both the House and Senate. This experience has given him firsthand knowledge of how to effectively advocate for the district.
about.bio4: When he's not advocating for education, you can find Mark on a golf course or playing music in his cover band: 45th Parallel.
about.schoolBoardText: Serving on the Hillsboro School District Board, advocating for students, teachers, and families across our community.
about.legislativeText: Working as a legislative aide in the State Capitol, learning firsthand how to effectively advocate for District 29 residents.
about.communityServiceText: Giving back to our neighbors through volunteer work and community involvement.
about.lifeText: An avid golfer and musician, Mark plays in his cover band 45th Parallel when not working for the community.
about.deeperDive1: I was born in Chicago but have always made my happiest homes where urban areas meet rural spaces. Maybe that's because my dad was born on a farm in rural Alabama. Although I grew up in the suburbs, my high school was surrounded on three sides by corn fields.
about.deeperDive2: In Tucson, AZ, where I received my Master's Degree at the University of Arizona and worked at the ABC affiliate KGUN, we lived on the far east side where the edge of Tucson meets the mountain wilderness. After a brief stop in Charlotte, NC, where I worked at the ABC affiliate WSOC and our daughter was born, I joined a team at Intel and we relocated to Oregon.
about.deeperDive3: We happily raised our children in the Hillsboro School District. In 2021 our home was taken from us in a fire and we had to start again. We remodeled the small home of my mother in downtown Hillsboro into a duplex, and now enjoy the many benefits of multigenerational living.
about.deeperDive4: After 26 years in Oregon, I hope we never have to live anywhere else.
about.ctaText: Support Mark's campaign for House District 29.

issues.heroSubtitle: From my years working on the school board and in legislative offices, I know passing policies or bills is a team effort. I also worked 15 seasons in Major League Baseball. These are the issues I will immediately get to work on with my colleagues.
issues.heroTagline: Put me in coach, I'm ready to play.
issues.educationText: Oregon's school funding formula is over 30 years old, and it shows. I'll advocate for a modernized funding model that uses broader outcome measures and accounts for the real costs facing districts like ours. Oregon's students deserve an approach built for the challenges they face today — not one designed for a problem we faced in the 1990s.
issues.economicText: Washington County is the economic engine of the state, but like all engines, it requires maintenance. We are facing a manufacturing recession, and the results are felt at every kitchen table. Let's pass policies that really illustrate that Oregon is open for business. Between the Forest Grove School District and Hillsboro School District there are over 50 Career and Technical Education pathways (CTE) that connect students with the business community to prepare the workforce of tomorrow.
issues.communityText: State Representatives should do more than vote on the House floor. It requires a proactive, visible presence in Cornelius, Forest Grove, and Hillsboro. The historical standard of an open door is insufficient in an era where constituents expect their leaders to be present in local spaces. I am committed to maintaining clear lines of communication with our city leaders and school districts to ensure our district's needs are never misinterpreted in Salem.
issues.ctaTitle: Ready to Build a Stronger District 29?
issues.ctaText: Support Mark Watson's campaign for Oregon House District 29.

contribute.heroSubtitle: Your support makes a difference. Help bring experienced, community-focused leadership to the Oregon House.
contribute.supportText: Every contribution - no matter the size - helps us reach more voters across Hillsboro, Cornelius, and Forest Grove. Your investment in this campaign is an investment in our community's future.
contribute.redirectNote: You will be redirected to our secure donation portal.
contribute.volunteerText: Want to volunteer, host a yard sign, or knock on doors? We'd love to hear from you.
contribute.grassrootsText: This campaign is powered by neighbors supporting neighbors. Every dollar helps us connect with more voters in our community.
contribute.outreachText: Your contribution funds door-to-door canvassing, mailers, and community events to share Mark's vision for District 29.
contribute.leadershipText: Back a candidate with real experience - school board service, legislative work, and deep roots in our community.
contribute.ctaText: Join the movement for experienced, community-focused leadership in Salem.
